verses.text: God has hardened Pharaoh that Israel may tell their children His signs. For the children. The wonders are to be remembered for generations.
God has hardened Pharaoh that Israel may tell their children His signs. For the children. The wonders are to be remembered for generations.
Moses warns of locusts to cover the land and eat what the hail left. Locusts threatened. Judgment will consume the remnant.
Moses warns of locusts to cover the land and eat what the hail left. Locusts threatened. Judgment will consume the remnant.
Moses warns of locusts to cover the land and eat what the hail left. Locusts threatened. Judgment will consume the remnant.
Moses warns of locusts to cover the land and eat what the hail left. Locusts threatened. Judgment will consume the remnant.
Pharaoh's servants plead; he offers to let only the men go, then drives Moses out. Half-offer. Partial obedience is no obedience.
Pharaoh's servants plead; he offers to let only the men go, then drives Moses out. Half-offer. Partial obedience is no obedience.
Pharaoh's servants plead; he offers to let only the men go, then drives Moses out. Half-offer. Partial obedience is no obedience.
Pharaoh's servants plead; he offers to let only the men go, then drives Moses out. Half-offer. Partial obedience is no obedience.
Pharaoh's servants plead; he offers to let only the men go, then drives Moses out. Half-offer. Partial obedience is no obedience.
Locusts darken the land and devour every green thing. Devoured. Egypt is stripped bare.
Locusts darken the land and devour every green thing. Devoured. Egypt is stripped bare.
Locusts darken the land and devour every green thing. Devoured. Egypt is stripped bare.
Locusts darken the land and devour every green thing. Devoured. Egypt is stripped bare.
Pharaoh hastily confesses his sin and begs forgiveness. Quick repentance. Pressure, not the heart, speaks.
Pharaoh hastily confesses his sin and begs forgiveness. Quick repentance. Pressure, not the heart, speaks.
Moses prays; a wind carries the locusts to the sea; but God hardens Pharaoh. Removed. The plague lifts, the heart does not.
Moses prays; a wind carries the locusts to the sea; but God hardens Pharaoh. Removed. The plague lifts, the heart does not.
Moses prays; a wind carries the locusts to the sea; but God hardens Pharaoh. Removed. The plague lifts, the heart does not.
The ninth plague — thick darkness three days; 'but all the children of Israel had light.' Darkness. Egypt is blind while Israel sees.
The ninth plague — thick darkness three days; 'but all the children of Israel had light.' Darkness. Egypt is blind while Israel sees.
The ninth plague — thick darkness three days; 'but all the children of Israel had light.' Darkness. Egypt is blind while Israel sees.
Pharaoh offers to let them go but keep the flocks; Moses refuses, 'not an hoof... left behind.' All or nothing. Worship requires our all.
Pharaoh offers to let them go but keep the flocks; Moses refuses, 'not an hoof... left behind.' All or nothing. Worship requires our all.
Pharaoh offers to let them go but keep the flocks; Moses refuses, 'not an hoof... left behind.' All or nothing. Worship requires our all.
Pharaoh, hardened, threatens Moses with death if he sees his face again. Last warning. The king shuts the door of mercy.
